trails.label: Town Trail
trails.desc: Voices, memories, everyday life — the Drogheda that people remember.

stops.name: The White Horse Hotel
stops.subtitle: West Street, Drogheda · now The Marcy Hotel
stops.desc: Where Brendan Behan held court

stops.name: Connolly's Shop
stops.subtitle: Shop Street, Drogheda · now Wogan Interiors
stops.desc: Commerce, community, memory

stops.name: Millmount
stops.subtitle: Millmount Square, Drogheda
stops.desc: The mound that remembers everything

stops.name: St Laurence's Gate
stops.subtitle: Laurence Street, Drogheda
stops.desc: The last gate of the walled town

stops.name: St Peter's Church
stops.subtitle: West Street, Drogheda
stops.desc: The National Shrine — links to the Plunkett Trail

stops.name: The Quayside
stops.subtitle: West Gate, River Boyne, Drogheda
stops.desc: The Steampacket era — links to the Diaspora Trail

trails.label: Plunkett Trail
trails.desc: A martyr's journey across Europe — from Drogheda to Ghent, Rome, London and beyond.

stops.name: The Quayside
stops.subtitle: West Gate, River Boyne, Drogheda
stops.desc: The departure point — following Plunkett out of Ireland

stops.name: St Peter's Church
stops.subtitle: West Street, Drogheda
stops.desc: His head rests here — the end of a journey that crossed a continent

stops.name: Loughcrew
stops.subtitle: Oldcastle, Co. Meath
stops.desc: Near the Plunkett family lands in Co. Meath

stops.name: Pontificio Collegio Irlandese
stops.subtitle: Rome, Italy
stops.desc: Where Plunkett studied and was ordained

stops.name: St Bavo's Cathedral
stops.subtitle: Ghent, Belgium
stops.desc: One of his European postings during exile

stops.name: Irish College
stops.subtitle: Louvain, Belgium
stops.desc: A key centre of Irish Catholic learning abroad

stops.name: Tyburn
stops.subtitle: London, England
stops.desc: Where he was tried and executed, 1681

stops.name: Benedictine Monastery
stops.subtitle: Lamspringe, Germany
stops.desc: Where his body rested for two centuries

trails.label: Diaspora Trail
trails.desc: Drogheda to Liverpool, Bordeaux and beyond — following the routes of those who left.

stops.name: The Quayside
stops.subtitle: West Gate, River Boyne, Drogheda
stops.desc: The point of departure for emigrants leaving by sea

stops.name: St Peter's Church
stops.subtitle: West Street, Drogheda
stops.desc: A place of farewell and last blessing before emigration

stops.name: Scotland Road
stops.subtitle: Vauxhall, Liverpool, England
stops.desc: The most common first landing point for Irish emigrants

stops.name: Irish College, Bordeaux
stops.subtitle: Rue du Hâ, Bordeaux, France
stops.desc: A wine trade destination with deep Irish connections

stops.name: Quai des Chartrons
stops.subtitle: Bordeaux, France
stops.desc: The quay where Irish merchant families built their trade

trails.label: Megalithic Trail
trails.desc: Five thousand years of continuous presence — Newgrange, Knowth, Dowth, Tara and Loughcrew.

stops.name: Millmount
stops.subtitle: Millmount Square, Drogheda
stops.desc: A Neolithic mound within the town itself — links to the Town Trail

stops.name: Newgrange
stops.subtitle: Brú na Bóinne, Co. Meath
stops.desc: Passage tomb older than Stonehenge — winter solstice aligned

stops.name: Knowth
stops.subtitle: Brú na Bóinne, Co. Meath
stops.desc: The largest passage tomb in Ireland, surrounded by satellite mounds

stops.name: Dowth
stops.subtitle: Brú na Bóinne, Co. Meath
stops.desc: The third great mound of the Brú na Bóinne complex

stops.name: Fourknocks
stops.subtitle: Stamullen, Co. Meath
stops.desc: A Neolithic passage tomb with remarkable carved stone art

stops.name: Hill of Tara
stops.subtitle: Co. Meath
stops.desc: The ancient seat of the High Kings of Ireland

stops.name: Loughcrew
stops.subtitle: Oldcastle, Co. Meath
stops.desc: A hilltop complex of passage tombs — the Hills of the Witch
